steps.title: Set off the appositive
steps.content: The phrase a country in Western Asia adds nonessential information about Bahrain. Because the sentence already has a dash after that phrase, the punctuation before the phrase must match it.

steps.title: Use matching dashes
steps.content: Choice B mixes a comma with a dash, so the interruption is not set off consistently. Choice C gives no punctuation before the appositive. Choice D uses a colon where the sentence needs punctuation to frame nonessential information. Matching dashes correctly produce Bahrain—a country in Western Asia—is. The answer is A.
